title: Whimsical Malaysian Fish Pasta
description: Tangy, dense Malaysian pasta with creamy coconut sauce, fresh fish fillet and herbs, egg‑free and adventurous.
instructions: Heat a pot of salted water and cook the spaghetti according to package directions until al dente; reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining.
In a small bowl, mix tamarind paste, lime juice, fish sauce, palm sugar and a pinch of salt until the sugar dissolves.
While the pasta cooks, heat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat and sauté minced garlic and sliced shallot until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Add the prepared tangy sauce mixture to the skillet and stir to combine, letting it simmer for 3 minutes to develop flavor.
Add the coconut milk, red chili flakes, and Thai basil, and heat the sauce until it starts to thicken slightly, about 2 minutes.
Add the fish fillet pieces to the sauce, cover, and simmer for 4–5 minutes until the fish is opaque and flakes easily with a fork.
Toss in the cooked spaghetti and a splash of reserved pasta water to create a smooth, creamy emulsion, stirring until the pasta is fully coated.
Adjust seasoning with additional fish sauce or lime juice if desired, then remove from heat.
Serve the pasta hot, garnished with extra basil leaves or a drizzle of coconut milk if preferred.
